SimpleSelect.description: Basic SELECT query: SELECT id, name FROM users
SimpleSelect.current_performance: ~470 ns/op on Apple M4; ~550-900 ns/op on CI (shared runners)
SimpleSelect.note: Benchmarks now use ParseFromModelTokens (production path) directly — eliminates the legacy token-wrapping overhead. Baseline set at 1.5x M4 measurement to accommodate CI runner variability.

ComplexQuery.description: Complex SELECT with JOIN, WHERE, ORDER BY, LIMIT
ComplexQuery.current_performance: ~2000 ns/op on Apple M4; ~2500-4000 ns/op on CI (shared runners)
ComplexQuery.note: Benchmarks now use ParseFromModelTokens (production path) directly. Baseline set at 1.5x M4 measurement; wide tolerance for CI runner variability.

WindowFunction.description: Window function query: ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY ... ORDER BY ...)
WindowFunction.current_performance: ~860 ns/op on Apple M4; ~1000-1500 ns/op on CI (shared runners)
WindowFunction.note: Benchmarks now use ParseFromModelTokens (production path) directly. Baseline set at 1.5x M4 measurement.

CTE.description: Common Table Expression with WITH clause
CTE.current_performance: ~800 ns/op on Apple M4; ~1000-1400 ns/op on CI (shared runners)
CTE.note: Benchmarks now use ParseFromModelTokens (production path) directly. Baseline set at 1.5x M4 measurement.

INSERT.current_performance: ~670 ns/op on Apple M4; ~750-1200 ns/op on CI (shared runners)
INSERT.note: Benchmarks now use ParseFromModelTokens (production path) directly. Baseline set at 1.5x M4 measurement.

TokenizationThroughput.description: Tokenizer throughput in tokens per second

EndToEndSustained.description: End-to-end sustained throughput in operations per second
